text: Wedge resection is one of the first core surgical procedures to master in thoracic surgery.
This model aims to train skills in VATS instrument handling, nodule localization, and resection.
To create the simulated lung nodule, select an appropriate area of the lung to implant the nodule.
Use surgical scissors of your choice to make an incision into the lung parenchyma through the visceral pleura.
Use blunt dissection to dilate the incision to create a pocket the size of a cherry tomato or a small grape.
Apply firm pressure for 2 minutes to ensure adhesion of the simulated lesion with the lung parenchyma.
Lastly, glue the track closed with the cyanoacrylate glue.
Repeat this process for 6 nodules in the following location.
First, have the trainees locate and expose each lung nodule with VATS instruments and isolate each nodule in turn.
Resect the target nodule with an endosapler with emphasis on using the appropriate reloads.
Then place the wedge specimen in the plastic bag and retrieve it via the working port.
